engineersdecor.config.title: Engineer's Decor Config
engineersdecor.tooltip.massive_speed_boo: Apply RF power to speed up.
engineersdecor.tooltip.slabpickup.help:
§rFast pickup by left-clicking while
 looking up/down and holding this slab.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.clinker_b:
A brick block with position
 dependent texture variations.
 Looks slightly darker and more
 color intensive than the
 vanilla brick block.

Quoin decoration for clinker
 wall accentuation. Place looking
 horizontally to generate a
 building corner ornamentation.
 Place while looking almost
 straight up/down for window
 ornamentation. The style of
 adjacent Sandstone Ornated
 Blocks are copied (preferred) to
 facilitate placements for
 higher buildings.

Slab made from a Clinker Block.
 Looks slightly darker and more
 color intensive than the
 vanilla brick.

A brick block with position
 dependent texture variations.
 Looks slightly darker and more
 color intensive than the vanilla
 brick block. Has more visible
 traces of grime or stain.
Slab made from a Stained Clinker Block.

Looks slightly darker and more
 color intensive than the vanilla
 brick block. Has more visible
 traces of grime or stain.

Looks slightly darker and more
 color intensive than the vanilla
 brick block.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.dark_shin:
Smoking chimney fitting to the
 Dark Shingle Roof Chimney Trunk.
 Click to change the smoke intensity.

Trunk to put a chimney on or
 place a IE Wire Connector to
 feed RF power into the
 building over the roof.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.dense_gri:
A compressed dirt-like soil,
 plain texture with some cracks.
 Known from often used paths or
 trampled yard grounds. Dirt and
 coarse dirt can be mixed in for
 accentuation. Position dependent
 texture variations. Inhibits
 grass spreading.

A compressed sandy soil mixed
 with stone grid, as known from
 machine and storage yards.
 Flowers don't really grow on
 that. Position dependent
 texture variations.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.factory_d:
Dropper suitable for advanced
 factory automation. Has twelve
 round-robin selected slots. Drop
 force, angle, stack size, and
 cool-down delay adjustable using
 sliders in the GUI. Three stack
 compare slots (below the
 inventory slots) with logical
 AND or OR can be used as internal
 trigger source. The internal
 trigger can be AND'ed or OR'ed
 with the external redstone signal
 trigger. Pre-opens shutter
 door when ready to drop and waiting
 for the external signal. Drops all
 matching stacks simultaneously.
 Hover buttons 2s for tooltips.

Drop direction x/y (-45° to +45°)§r
§7See the green-red coordinate system at the front.

External signal combination logic§r
§9AND§7: Drop only on an external signal.
§9OR§7: Drop filter matches automatically, non-matching items on external trigger.
§9IGNORE§7: Completely ignores external Redstone (permanent external trigger).

Filter combination gate logic§r
§9AND§7: Drop the stacks as specified in the filters when ALL filters are green.
§9OR§7: Drop the stack of each individual filter when green.

External Redstone signal indicator
§7(click to generate a manual trigger).

Trigger mode§r
§9Pulse§7: One drop for each OFF->ON of the external signal.
§9Continuous§7: Drop as long as the external signal is ON.

Drop velocity (slow to fast speed)
§7Spring bias/prestress low at the bottom, high at the top position.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.factory_h:
Hopper suitable for advanced factory
 automation. Can transfer half-stacks, max
 collection range 9x9. GUI Slider controls:
 - Collection range (0 to 4)
 - Insertion delay (0.5s to 10s)
 - Insertion stack size (1 to 32).
 GUI Redstone controls:
 Not inverted / inverted (default),
 pulse mode / continuous mode (default).

Delay indicator
§7Blinks when the insertion delay timer has not expired yet, or when the Hopper cannot insert into the adjacent inventory.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.factory_p:
Allows placing blocks and planting
 crops or trees. GUI Redstone controls:
 Not inverted / inverted (default),
 pulse mode / continuous mode (default).
 Crop seeds plant into clear space
 above farmland. Spits out items
 that it cannot place or plant.
External signal §7(click to run one placement cycle)

Stock row 3§r
§7Click to start placement from the bottom storage row.

Stock row 2§r
§7Click to start placement from the middle storage row.

Stock row 1§r
§7Click to start placement from the top storage row.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.fluid_bar:
Simple barrel for storing liquids.
 Has a small built-in pressure-tube
 based level gauge. Default placement
 is standing, sneak-place to
 select a specific direction.
 Transfers fluids in tanks below
 when standing. Supports fluid
 extraction/insertion as item
Supports Comparator output.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.halfslab_:
Vertically stackable slice.
 Right/left click with the slice
 stack on the top or bottom surface
 to add/remove slices.

Vertically stackable slice.
 Right/left click with the slice
 stack on the top or bottom surface
 to add/remove slices.

Vertically stackable slice.
 Right/left click with the slice

 to add/remove slices.

Vertically stackable slice.

block.engineers_decor_reforged.iron_bulb:
Small glowstone light source,
 placed on the ceiling, walls,
 fence posts or metal poles.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.iron_ceil:
Small glowstone light source,
 placed at the edge of a
 ceiling block.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.iron_floo:
Small glowstone light source,
 placed at the edge of a floor
 block. Useful to light up places
 where electrical light
 installations are problematic.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.iron_hatc:
An upside down trapdoor, placed
 above maintenance shafts or ladders.
 Creeper-proof, works as ladder
 when open.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.iron_inse:
Small glowstone light source, sunk
 into the floor, ceiling or wall.
 Useful to light up places where
 electrical light installations
 are problematic.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.metal_run:
Typical industrial wall ladder,
 consisting of horizontal metal
 rod rungs. Look up/down to climb
 faster.

Staggered rod rungs affixed to a
 wall, allowing to climb up, fall
 down, and so on. Look up/down
 to climb faster.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.panzergla:
Reinforced glass block.
 Expensive, explosion-proof.
 Dark gray tint, faint structural
 lines visible, multi texture for
 seamless look.

Reinforced glass slab.
 Expensive, explosion-proof.
 Dark gray tint, faint structural
 lines visible.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.rebar_con:
Steel reinforced concrete block.
 Expensive but Creeper/Wither-proof.

Steel reinforced concrete slab.
 Expensive but Creeper/Wither-proof.

Steel reinforced concrete stairs.
 Expensive but Creeper/Wither-proof.

Steel reinforced concrete tile.
 Expensive but Creeper-proof.

Steel reinforced concrete tile slab.
 Expensive but Creeper-proof.

Steel reinforced concrete tile stairs.
 Expensive but Creeper-proof.

Steel reinforced concrete wall.
 Expensive but Creeper-proof.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.sign_deco: Plain industrial sign plate for workshop labels, display walls, and factory markings.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.sign_hotw:
Electrical hazard warning.
 Don't forget to place around HV,
 or you will have a nonconformity
 in the next audit.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.slag_bric:
A gray-brown brick block with position
 dependent texture variations.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.small_blo: Breaks blocks in front of its working face. Can be disabled by applying a redstone signal. Processing speed depends on the target block.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.small_ele:
Small metal cased pass-through furnace.
 Can draw items from the input side,
 puts items into the inventory at
 the output side. Items can be
 inserted or drawn from all sides
 using hoppers. Implicitly bypasses
 items that cannot be smelted or
 cooked. Slightly more energy
 efficient and faster than a heated
 cobblestone furnace. Fifos and
 feeders transfer whole stacks.
 Feeders require a bit of power.
 Place a Hopper into an aux slot
 to automatically draw items from
 inventories on the input side.

Auxiliary slot
§7Place a Hopper here to enable automatic feeding from the input side.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.small_flu:
Collects fluids above it. Has an
 internal tank with three buckets
 capacity. Traces flowing fluids
 to nearby source blocks. The
 fluid can be obtained with fluid
 transfer systems or a bucket.
 Fills only tanks below (gravity
 transfer). Compatible with vanilla
 infinite-water-source creation.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.small_fre:
Ice production device. Cools down
 water to ice, packed ice, and
 finally blue ice. Click with a
 water bucket or pipe in water and
 ensure the device is RF powered.
 Use a Hopper or other item
 extraction to retrieve the ice
 variant. Remove the RF power or
 apply a redstone signal to disable
 the freezer. For automation, you
 can use a redstone comparator to
 detect which ice phase is
 currently present.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.small_lab:
Small metal cased lab kiln. Solid
 fuel consuming, updraught. Slightly
 hotter and better isolated than a
 cobblestone furnace, therefore
 more efficient. Two auxiliary
 slots e.g. for storage. Two stack
 internal hopper fifos for input,
 output, and fuel. Place an
 external heater into a aux slot
 and connect power for electrical
 smelting speed boost.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.small_mil:
Occasionally milks cows. Has an
 internal fluid tank. Does not
 feed the animals. Use buckets to
 retrieve the milk. Pulls/stores
 milk container items from/to
 inventories at the back or bottom
 (prefers extracting from the
 back and inserting below, but can
 also put filled vessels back into
 the same inventory). Supports fluid
 output to tanks or pipes below
 (only if milk exists as fluid).
 Care that it's not too crowdy in
 the cow pen, only happy animals
 stroll by voluntarily.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.small_min:
High temperature, high insulation
 electrical stone melting furnace.§r
Heats up mineral blocks to magma
 blocks, and finally to lava.
 Click with a mineral block (stone,
 cobble, etc) or use item insertion
 to place a block in the melter.
 Use bucket or fluid extraction
 to retrieve the lava. When cooling
 down lava, obsidian is generated.
 Remove the RF power or apply a
 redstone signal to disable the
 furnace. For automation, use a
 redstone comparator to see which
 melting phase the mineral is in.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.small_sol:
Produces a small amount of power
 when exposed to sunlight. Useful
 for charging LF capacitors in remote
 systems with low consumption. The
 internal charge pump circuit
 accumulates and frequently
 transfers RF. Production depends
 on day time and the weather.
Click to see State Of Charge,
 production, and progress.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.small_tre: Cuts grown trees in front of its working face. It does not collect the drops. Disable it with a redstone signal.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.small_was:
Trash with internal fifo slots.
 Items can be inserted on all sides,
 and are kept until there is no
 space left in the fifo. After that
 the oldest stack will be incinerated.
 Apply electrical RF/FE power to
 increase the processing speed.
 Keeps its inventory when being
 relocated.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.steel_cat:
Simple catwalk for better access to
big machines. Can also be placed in
look direction from the top. Click
with a Railing to add the guardrail
to a side (close click: exact
placement, far click: auto best
match). Click again to remove a
guardrail.

Click with a Steel Railing to add
or remove guardrails. Cannot be
placed bottom-down, no corners
(no 'normal' Stairs).

Top aligned Catwalk, connects to
Steel poles below.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.steel_flo:
Decorative steel floor covering.
 Top aligned. Items fall through.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.steel_fra:
Steel framed triple glazed window.
 Well insulating. Does not connect
 to adjacent blocks like glass panes.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.steel_mes:
Industrial style fence.
 Does not connect to regular fences.
 Connects to full blocks if in line
 with another mesh fence.

Industrial style fence gate that
 fits well to the Steel Mesh Fence.
 Can be placed as single or double
 size gate.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.steel_rai:
Decorative guardrail. Add or remove
sides by clicking while holding
railings in your hand. Also works
on Steel Catwalks.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.straight_:
Straight fluid pipe fragment.
 Conducts fluids only in one direction.
 Does not connect to the sides.
 Reduces flow rate. Sneak to place
 in reverse direction.

Straight fluid pipe fragment.
 Conducts fluids only in one direction.
 Does not connect to the sides.
 Sneak to place in reverse
 direction. Blocks if not redstone
 powered.

Straight fluid pipe fragment.
 Conducts fluids only in one direction.
 Does not connect to the sides.
 Sneak to place in reverse direction.
 Blocks if not redstone powered,
 reduces the flow rate linear from
 power 1 to 14, opens to maximum
 possible valve flow rate for
 power 15.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.thick_ste:
Straight hollow pole fragment
 (6x6x16) for structural support
 purposes.

Steel part fitting as foot or head
 of the thick steel pole (6x6x16).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.thin_stee:
Straight hollow pole fragment
 (4x4x16) for structural support
 purposes.

Steel part fitting as foot or head
 of the thin steel pole (4x4x16).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.treated_w: Decorative floor tiles with texture variations.

Straight pole fragment with a
 diameter of a wire relay. Can
 be useful as alternative to the
 wire posts if special lengths
 are needed, or as support for structures.

Wooden part fitting as foot or
 head of straight poles.

Heavy duty wooden support part
 fitting as foot or head of straight
 poles.

Robust Wood Stool. Indoor and
 outdoor use.
rsgauges.config.title: Redstone Controls Config
block.engineers_decor_reforged.arrow_tar:
A target that emits a redstone
 pulse to the block it is attached
 to when it is hit with an arrow
 or activated by hand.
 Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.door_sens:
A player detector that is mounted
 on walls above doors.§r
block.engineers_decor_reforged.elevator_:
Points up, down, or in both directions
depending on where you click the wall
when placing.
A switch that outputs a redstone
 signal for a short time, like a
 button.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_but:
A switch that outputs a redstone
 signal for a short time, like a
 button.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Activate while already active to
 extend the pulse time.
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Left-click with any dye to color
 tint the switch.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_con:
A glass floor plate that changes
 color and emits a redstone signal
 when exposed to mechanical
 pressure on the top (e.g. someone
 standing on it), and a short time
 after the pressure was released.
 Outputs are to the front and
 bottom. Does not emit any sounds.
 Sensors are high sensitive.
 Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Left-click with any dye to color
 tint the switch.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_day:
A small glass device that emits a
 redstone signal from a configured
 start (day) time to a configured
 end time. The time span is set
 with buttons on the device front
 face (24 hour format, where 06:00
 is sunrise). These buttons also set
 output power and a
 randomizing value. The latter setting
 delays switching on or off for
 a random time span, where higher
 values cause longer delays. Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on. Does not emit switching
 sounds.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_doo:
A glass floor plate that changes
 color and emits a redstone signal
 when exposed to mechanical
 pressure on the top (e.g. someone
 standing on it), and a short time
 after the pressure was released.
 Outputs are to the front and
 bottom. Does not emit any sounds.
 Click on the touch config up/down
 buttons to set output power,
 entity filter, entity count,
 and sensitivity.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Left-click with any dye to color
 tint the switch.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_ent:
A small glass device that
 observes a defined volume and
 emits a redstone signal if it
 sees an entity. The volume is two
 blocks above and below, and 'range'
 in front, left and right (covers
 180 degrees). The range can be
 configured with small buttons on
 the front face, together with the
 output strength, the number of
 entities seen, and the entity type
 seen (mobs, players, animals, etc).
 Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on. Does not
 emit switching sounds.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_int:
Emits a signal with a defined on-
 and off-time. Configured using
 buttons on the front:
 - §1Blue: On-time§r
- §6Yellow: Off-time§r
- §2Green: Signal ramp up/down§r
- §4Red: Output power§r
 The timer is placed in standby
 mode for setup. Click on the front
 face to set it into run mode. Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
 As Switch Link target the timer
 toggles between run and standby
 mode.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_lin:
A small glass device that observes
 in one direction and emits a
 redstone signal if it senses an
 entity. The observed volume is
 'range' blocks in front, and half
 a block to the sides. The range
 can be configured with small
 buttons on the front face, together
 with the output strength, the
 number of entities seen, and the
 entity type seen (mobs, players,
 animals, etc). Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on. Does not emit switching
 sounds. Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_rot:
A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with any dye to color
 tint the switch.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_sma:
A switch that outputs a redstone
 signal for a short time, like a
 button.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Activate while already active to
 extend the pulse time.
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Left-click with any dye to color
 tint the switch.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_tou:
A switch that outputs a redstone
 signal for a short time, like a
 button.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Activate while already active to
 extend the pulse time.
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Left-click with any dye to color
 tint the switch.

A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with any dye to color
 tint the switch.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.glass_ver:
A display device that can be
 attached to a solid block to
 visualize the redstone power of
 that block. If the block can
 provide power itself, the gauge
 uses the output of it, otherwise
 the gauge will indirectly measure
 the power that the block receives
 from adjacent blocks. Strong and
 weak power is supported. Indirect
 weak measurements may have a
 short latency.
block.engineers_decor_reforged.industria: Alarm Lamp

A red lamp that blinks when
 detecting a redstone signal from
 the block it is attached to.
 Emits light when active.
Alarm Siren

A siren that frequently emits an
 alarm sound when detecting a
 redstone signal from the block
 it is attached to.
Analog Voltmeter Redstone Gauge

A display device that can be
 attached to a solid block to
 visualize the redstone power of
 that block. If the block can
 provide power itself, the gauge
 uses the output of it, otherwise
 the gauge will indirectly measure
 the power that the block receives
 from adjacent blocks. Strong and
 weak power is supported. Indirect
 weak measurements may have a
 short latency.
Small Analog Voltmeter Redstone Gauge

A display device that can be
 attached to a solid block to
 visualize the redstone power of
 that block. If the block can
 provide power itself, the gauge
 uses the output of it, otherwise
 the gauge will indirectly measure
 the power that the block receives
 from adjacent blocks. Strong and
 weak power is supported. Indirect
 weak measurements may have a
 short latency.
Industrial Block Category Detector

Emits a redstone signal if one or
 more blocks in front of it match a
 configured category. Configure
 with the buttons at the back side:
 - §1Range§r (observing how far)
 - §6Threshold§r (how many matches)
 - §3Debouncing§r (anti-jitter delay)
 §4- Output power§r
 - §2Filter§r (solid, fluid, wood log)
 Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Machine Button

A switch that outputs a redstone
 signal for a short time, like a
 button.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Activate while already active to
 extend the pulse time.
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Can be activated with projectiles.
Industrial Comparator Switch

A compact sensor switch that
 checks the block it is mounted on.
 If the attached block has
 comparator output, inventory
 contents, or redstone power, the
 switch turns on and emits
 full-strength redstone.
 Mount it on storage, tanks, or
 redstone hardware to convert the
 attached signal into a simple
 on/off control line.
Industrial Contact Mat

An industrial floor contact mat
 that emits a redstone signal when
 exposed to mechanical pressure
 on the top side (e.g. someone
 standing on it), and a short time
 after the pressure was released.
 Outputs are to the front and bottom.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Industrial Day Timer Clock

A small device that emits a
 redstone signal from a configured
 start (day) time to a configured
 end time. The time span is set
 with buttons on the device front
 face (24 hour format, where 06:00
 is sunrise). These buttons also set
 output power and a
 randomizing value. The latter setting
 delays switching on or off for
 a random time span, where higher
 values cause longer delays. Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Industrial Dimmer

An industrial manual redstone
 power dimmer. Lets you manually
 set an output signal between 0
 and 15 at the back side of the
 device. The vertical click position
 defines the signal strength.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Industrial Door Contact Mat

An industrial floor contact mat
 that emits a redstone signal when
 exposed to mechanical pressure on
 the top (e.g. someone standing on
 it), and a short time after the
 pressure was released. Outputs
 are to the front and bottom.
Click on the touch config up/down
 buttons to set output power,
 entity filter, entity count,
 and sensitivity.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Industrial Button

A switch that outputs a redstone
 signal for a short time, like a
 button.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Activate while already active to
 extend the pulse time.
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Industrial Infrared Entity Detector

A small device that observes a
 defined volume and emits a
 redstone signal if it sees an
 entity. The volume is two blocks
 above and below, and 'range' in
 front, left and right (covers
 180 degrees). The range can be
 configured with small buttons on
 the front face, together with the
 output strength, the number of
 entities seen, and the entity
 type seen (mobs, players, animals,
 etc). Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
ESTOP Button

A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
 Can be shot off with a projectile.
Industrial Fall-Through Detection Frame

An industrial sensor frame that
 emits a redstone signal when
 something falls through it. Output
 is to the block it is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Fenced Machine Button

A switch that outputs a redstone
 signal for a short time, like a
 button.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Activate while already active to
 extend the pulse time.
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Industrial Foot Button

A switch that outputs a redstone
 signal for a short time, like a
 button.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Activate while already active to
 extend the pulse time.
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Green Blinking LED Redstone Indicator

A small device that can be
 attached to a block to indicate
 if that block is powered. If the
 block can provide power itself,
 it uses the output of the block,
 otherwise it looks up the power
 that the block receives from
 adjacent blocks. Left click with
 dye to change the LED color.
 Blinks with a frequency of about 0.5Hz.
Green LED Redstone Indicator

A small device that can be
 attached to a block to indicate
 if that block is powered. If the
 block can provide power itself,
 it uses the output of the block,
 otherwise it looks up the power
 that the block receives from
 adjacent blocks. Left click with
 dye to change the LED color.
Industrial Sensitive Shock Activated Trapdoor

An industrial floor trap door
 that swings open and emits a
 redstone signal when someone or
 something falls on it, or when
 someone walks on it without
 sneaking. Output is to the block
 it is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Hopper Blocker

A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Can be placed on hoppers.
Industrial Interval Signal Timer

Emits a signal with a defined on-
 and off-time. Configured using
 buttons on the front:
 - §1Blue: On-time§r
- §6Yellow: Off-time§r
- §2Green: Signal ramp up/down§r
- §4Red: Output power§r
 The timer is placed in standby
 mode for setup. Click on the front
 face to set it into run mode. Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
 As Switch Link target the timer
 toggles between run and standby
 mode.
Knock Detector Pulse Switch (industrial)

Emits a redstone pulse when the
 adjacent block of its facing is
 clicked.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Activate while already active to
 extend the pulse time.
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Knock Detector Bistable Switch (industrial)

Switches on/off when the adjacent
 block of its facing is clicked.§r
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Industrial Lever

A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Industrial Local Light Sensor

A small device that measures the
 local light level at its location
 and emits a redstone signal
 based on configured on/off
 thresholds set with small
 buttons on the front face.§r These buttons also set
 output power and debouncing. Debouncing
 delays switching off or on until
 the light level is stable, higher
 values mean better noise
 filtering, but also more switching
 delay. If on and off settings are
 identical, then the measured
 light level must exactly match
 the set value. Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Industrial Thunderstorm Detector

A small wall mounted device that
 emits a redstone signal when it
 senses a high electric potential
 in the air, i.e. in thunder
 storms.§r The buttons on the front
 face configure the
 output signal strength. Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Industrial Linear Laser Entity Detector

A small device that observes in
 one direction and emits a redstone
 signal if it senses an entity.§r
The observed volume is 'range'
 blocks in front, and half a block
 to the sides. The range can be
 configured with small buttons on
 the front face, together with the
 output strength, the number of
 entities seen, and the entity
 type seen (mobs, players, animals,
 etc). Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Two-Button Machine Switch

A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Industrial Pull Handle Button

A switch that outputs a redstone
 signal for a short time, like a
 button.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Activate while already active to
 extend the pulse time.
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Industrial Rain Sensor

A small wall mounted device that
 emits a redstone signal when it
 rains on it. The buttons on the
 front face configure
 the output signal strength.
 Output is sent to the block
 the sensor is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Red LED Redstone Indicator

A small device that can be
 attached to a block to indicate
 if that block is powered. If the
 block can provide power itself,
 it uses the output of the block,
 otherwise it looks up the power
 that the block receives from
 adjacent blocks. Left click with
 dye to change the LED color.
 Blinks with a frequency of about 0.5Hz.
Red LED Redstone Indicator

A small device that can be
 attached to a block to indicate
 if that block is powered. If the
 block can provide power itself,
 it uses the output of the block,
 otherwise it looks up the power
 that the block receives from
 adjacent blocks. Left click with
 dye to change the LED color.

A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Can be placed on hoppers.
Rotary Machine Switch

A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Industrial Shock Sensitive Contact Mat

An industrial contact mat emitting
 a redstone pulse when exposed to
 mechanical shock from above (e.g.
 something falls on it).
 Outputs are to the front and
 bottom. Power is always 15.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Industrial Shock Activated Trapdoor

An industrial floor trap door
 that swings open and emits a
 redstone signal when someone or
 something falls on it. Output is
 to the block it is mounted on.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set the hold-time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Small Digital Display Redstone Gauge

A display device that can be
 attached to a solid block to
 visualize the redstone power of
 that block. If the block can
 provide power itself, the gauge
 uses the output of it, otherwise
 the gauge will indirectly measure
 the power that the block receives
 from adjacent blocks. Strong and
 weak power is supported. Indirect
 weak measurements may have a
 short latency.
Industrial Contact Lever

A switch that is manually flipped
 on or off like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Industrial Cased Pulse Switch Link Receiver

A full block industrial pulse
 switching device that is optimized
 to be used as a Switch Link
 target. Like a normal pulse switch
 it activates for a short time when
 manually clicked or triggered by a
 switch link. Click with an Ender
 Pearl to create a linked pearl.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Industrial Cased Switch Link Receiver

A full block cased industrial
 bistable switching device that is
 optimized to be used as a Switch
 Link target. Click with an Ender
 Pearl to create a linked pearl.
 Can also be manually activated
 like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Industrial Pulse Switch Link Receiver

A small industrial pulse switching
 device that is optimized to be
 used as a Switch Link target. Like
 a normal pulse switch it activates
 for a short time when manually
 clicked or triggered by a switch
 link. Click with an Ender Pearl
 to create a linked pearl.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Left-click with a stack of
 redstone to set a fixed pulse time
 between 0.1s (1) and 6.4s (64).
Industrial Switch Link Pulse Transmitter

A relay-styled industrial control
 fitting for redstone panels. It
 behaves as a compact manual toggle.
 Use Switch Link receivers when
 you need remote pearl activation.
Industrial Switch Link Receiver

A small industrial bistable
 switching device that is optimized
 to be used as a Switch Link target.
 Click with an Ender Pearl to
 create a linked pearl. Can also
 be manually activated like a lever.
Click with a Redstone Torch to
 configure the output (cycles
 through the options that this
 switch supports).
Industrial Analog Switch Link Receiver

A linked receiver for control
 panels. Click with an Ender Pearl
 to create a linked pearl; triggering
 the pearl emits a full redstone
 signal at the receiver.
Industrial Switch Link Transmitter

A relay-styled industrial control
 fitting for redstone panels. It
 behaves as a compact manual toggle
 for local checks. Use Switch Link
 receivers when you need remote
 pearl activation.
Industrial Analog Switch Link Transmitter

A relay-styled control fitting for
 panel layouts. Use receivers and
 linked pearls for direct remote
 activation.
Glass Tube Redstone Gauge

A display device that can be

 the gauge will indirectly measure
 the power that the block receives
 from adjacent blocks. Strong and
 weak power is supported. Indirect
 weak measurements may have a
 short latency.

item.engineers_decor_reforged.switchlink:
Create it by clicking a Switch
 Link receiver with an Ender Pearl.
 Sneak-use the linked pearl to
 remotely activate the stored
 receiver. The target must be
 loaded, in the same dimension,
 and within range.
item.engineers_decor_reforged.ariadne_co:
Reusable coal marker for placing
 8-way black arrow route marks
 on stable mine faces.
No stable surface for that marker.
item.engineers_decor_reforged.charged_la:
Stores one experience level.
 Consume to retrieve the level,
 remove active effects, and recover
 a small amount of health.

Creates Charged Lapis from one
 lapis lazuli and one experience
 level. A successful squeeze hurts,
 exhausts, and briefly blinds the user.
item.engineers_decor_reforged.crushing_h:
Manual handheld ore crushing
 hammer. Craft with raw iron, raw
 gold, or matching ores to make
 coarse grit. The hammer takes
 durability damage and is not
 consumed until it breaks.
item.engineers_decor_reforged.diving_cap:
Restores air when you are about
 to drown. It works from inventory
 with a cooldown and can also be
 used manually.
Can be smelted or blasted into
 gold ingots.
Can be smelted or blasted into
 iron ingots.
item.engineers_decor_reforged.material_b:
Portable single-material storage.
 Hold the box in the main hand and
 a stack in the offhand to store it;
 use with empty offhand to retrieve.
Hold the material box in the main hand.
This box already holds a different material.
Modified stacks cannot be stored safely.
Empty the offhand before retrieving material.
Retrieved %1$s items from the material box.
Stored %1$s items in the material box.
That item cannot be stored in the material box.
Fast nutritional snack made from
 seeds, fruit, and bread.
item.engineers_decor_reforged.musli_bar_:
Portable food press. Use with
 bread, an apple, and wheat seeds
 in inventory to press four power
 bars.
Need bread, an apple, and wheat seeds.
Pressed a batch of Muslee bars.
item.engineers_decor_reforged.redia_tool:
Diamond-covered multi-tool.
 Use with torches to place one;
 sneak-use to shear, cycle soil,
 strip logs, or fell trees.
The REDIA Tool has no useful work there.
No torch available for marker placement.
item.engineers_decor_reforged.sleeping_b:
Weatherproof portable rest kit.
 Use it at night in the Overworld
 to skip to morning without setting
 a spawn point.
It is not late enough to rest.
This place will not hold a safe rest.
It is not safe enough to rest.
item.engineers_decor_reforged.stimpack.h:
Automatically injects when health
 is low, healing, protecting, and
 briefly boosting movement with a
 cooldown. Can also be used manually.
item.engineers_decor_reforged.tracker.he:
Click a block to store its location.
 Sneak-use to clear the target. The
 tooltip reports the saved dimension
 and coordinates.
